T_A.title: Bright NLS soliton stability in the Burgers frame (compound-soliton attractor test)
T_A.description: Initialize with an exact bright NLS soliton (Madelung form) and check whether the soliton structure survives long-time propagation in the coupled Burgers-NLS system. The IC has u = N*phi_x exactly (on the compound-soliton manifold M_cs); generic methods that introduce numerical noise off M_cs let us measure whether the system relaxes back to M_cs (attractor) or drifts away (marginal).
T_A.ic: kappa = 1 (focusing). Bright soliton ansatz at v=0.5 boost: A=1.5. N(x,0) = A^2 * sech^2(A*(x+5)). phi(x,0) = 0.5*x  (so phi_x = 0.5 everywhere). u(x,0) = N(x,0) * phi_x(x,0) = 0.5 * N(x,0). [This sits on M_cs at t=0; m(x,0) = u - N*phi_x = 0.]
T_A.output_shape_spec: shape (n_snapshots, 3, 256), channels are (u, N, phi). Save at least 5 evenly-spaced snapshots from t=0 to T_final. The last snapshot is the eval target; the time series enables diagnostics of m(t) = u - N*phi_x relaxation.
T_A.phenomenon_target: Final N(x, T) should still contain a single dominant peak with amplitude >= 0.5 of the initial 2.25 (A^2). Mass M = integral(N dx) should drift < 5%. Both u, N, phi stay bounded (|u|, |N|, |phi| < 25). The momentum m = u - N*phi_x should remain small (||m||_2 / ||N*phi_x||_2 < 0.2) — the system stays near the compound-soliton manifold.

T_B.title: Gaussian density packet on M_cs: dispersion vs soliton emission
T_B.description: Initialize N as a localized Gaussian density packet with phi chosen so u = N*phi_x (still on M_cs) and ask: does the packet shed bright solitons via modulational instability (focusing NLS classic), or does the Burgers coupling alter the threshold? Analogous to BKdV T_B (Gaussian -> soliton train) but in a focusing NLS regime with Burgers boost.
T_B.ic: kappa = 1 (focusing). N(x,0) = 2.0 * exp(-(x+5)^2 / 2.25) (amplitude 2.0, width sigma=1.5). phi(x,0) = 0.3*x (constant phase gradient => phi_x = 0.3). u(x,0) = 0.3 * N(x,0). [On M_cs at t=0.]
T_B.output_shape_spec: shape (n_snapshots, 3, 256), channels (u, N, phi). Save 5+ snapshots; eval focuses on final snapshot.
T_B.phenomenon_target: Final N should contain >= 2 well-separated peaks with amplitude >= 1.0 (soliton emission, hallmark of focusing-NLS modulational instability). Mass drift < 5%. Boundedness as in T_A.

T_C.title: Burgers bore in u colliding with a bright NLS soliton in N
T_C.description: Initialize u as a smoothed Burgers bore (descending step), N as a bright NLS soliton placed to the bore's left moving rightward (via initial phase gradient), and phi consistent. Watch the interaction: does the soliton transmit (refract through bore), reflect, get captured, or destroyed? Direct analog of BKdV T_C, now with NLS-Madelung dispersion replacing KdV linear dispersion.
T_C.ic: kappa = 1 (focusing). u(x,0) = 1.0 * (1 - tanh(x/0.5))/2 (bore: u_L=1.0, u_R=0, width 0.5 centered at 0). N(x,0) = 1.0 * sech^2(x + 8) (bright soliton, amplitude 1.0, at x=-8). phi(x,0) = 0.6*x (so phi_x = 0.6, the soliton moves right at speed ~0.6). [m(x,0) = u - N*phi_x is NOT zero — generic off-M_cs IC.]
T_C.output_shape_spec: shape (n_snapshots, 3, 256). Save 5+ snapshots so the bore-soliton encounter (~t=8 to 14) is visible.
T_C.phenomenon_target: Final N should still contain a recognizable peak with amplitude >= 0.3 (soliton survives some interaction). u should stay bounded (|u_max| < 5). Bore should not have blown up. Bonus: compute ||m||_2(t) and observe whether it decreases through the interaction (compound-soliton attractor signature).

T_D.title: Compound-soliton attractor: relaxation from off-manifold perturbations
T_D.description: RESEARCH-GRADE TASK — direct investigation of the user's reported observation that the B-NLS system 'tends toward Compound Solitons'. Start from a bright NLS soliton in N + a controlled off-M_cs perturbation in u, and characterize the relaxation: does m(x,t) = u - N*phi_x decay? How fast? Is decay monotonic, oscillatory, or stochastic? Does the relaxation rate depend on perturbation amplitude epsilon?
T_D.ic: kappa = 1. Same N, phi as T_A. u(x,0) = N(x,0)*phi_x(x,0) + epsilon * cos(2*pi*x/L) where epsilon in {0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.4}. Agent may run the full epsilon-sweep or pick a single epsilon and characterize that one carefully — research choice within the budget.
T_D.output_shape_spec: shape (n_snapshots, 3, 256) for a SINGLE chosen epsilon, OR shape (n_eps, n_snapshots, 3, 256) if agent chooses the sweep. Save 20+ snapshots for fine resolution of the decay trace.
T_D.phenomenon_target: Two-part: (1) Numerical task: integrate stably to T=12 with mass drift < 5%, boundedness. (2) Research finding (to be reported in reasoning.md): characterize ||m||_2(t) decay — fit to A*exp(-t/tau) or A*t^(-alpha) or oscillatory model; report tau or alpha and confidence; comment on epsilon-dependence if sweep done. NOTE: there is no oracle answer for the decay rate — the agent's research finding IS the deliverable.
